perturbation.original_text: 1.11 "Control" or "Controlled" shall mean possession of the ability to grant a license or sublicense as provided for herein without violating the terms of an agreement with a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.11 "Control" or "Controlled" shall mean possession of the ability to grant a license or sublicense as provided for herein, even when violating the terms of an agreement with a third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction because Section 1.11, which initially defines 'Control' as not violating third-party agreements, now states it can even when violating such agreements. This creates ambiguity in scenarios where FibroGen grants a license that inadvertently breaches an existing agreement with another company. This contradicts implicit expectations of lawful licensing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 13 LICENSE GRANTS 13.1 Grant to Astellas. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement including Article 12 above, FG hereby grants to Astellas an exclusive license under the FG Technology to: use, package, sell, have sold, import, market and otherwise distribute the Lead Compounds for use solely in the Field in the Astellas Territory
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the expectation of lawful licensing implied within Article 13.1. Initially, Section 1.11 defined 'Control' as the ability to grant licenses without violating third-party agreements. The modification now allows 'Control' even when violating such agreements. This creates a direct conflict with Article 13.1, which grants Astellas an exclusive license 'subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.' If FG grants a license to Astellas that violates another agreement, it breaches the initial terms, rendering Article 13.1 unenforceable and creating ambiguity in Astellas's licensed rights. This inconsistency regarding Astellas's granted license constitutes a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.10 Warranty. FG represents and warrants that the Lead Compounds to be supplied to Astellas under this Agreement shall conform to the Product Specifications and shall, as appropriate, be manufactured in compliance with GMP Guidelines. Subject to Sections 12.9 and 17.3 hereof, FG's sole obligation and Astellas' sole remedy with respect to Lead Compound which does not meet the warranty contained herein is limited to replacement of such Lead Compound and reimbursement of Astellas' out of pocket expenses for shipping to FG at the address designated by FG.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.10 Warranty. FG represents and warrants that the Lead Compounds to be supplied to Astellas under this Agreement shall generally conform to the Product Specifications and shall, as appropriate, be manufactured with reasonable compliance with GMP Guidelines. Subject to Sections 12.9 and 17.3 hereof, FG's sole obligation and Astellas' sole remedy with respect to Lead Compound which does not meet the warranty contained herein includes replacement of such Lead Compound and reimbursement of Astellas' out of pocket expenses for shipping to FG at the address designated by FG, or if such is not feasible for FG, any other remedy as chosen by Astellas.
perturbation.explanation: The initial warranty specified strict adherence to Product Specifications and GMP Guidelines, but the modified version introduces ambiguity by stating 'generally conform' and 'reasonable compliance.' This means a deviation can occur. Additionally, adding 'any other remedy as chosen by Astellas,' broadens the remedies beyond the original 'replacement and reimbursement,' creating a contradiction since in the first version only replacement and reimbursement was specified and not any other remedy that can be chosen by Astellas. This affects the enforcibility of warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.3 Disclaimer of Warranties. EXCEPT AS OTHERWISE SET FORTH HEREIN, FG AND ASTELLAS EXPRESSLY DISCLAIM ANY WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS, EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY OR OTHERWISE, WITH RESPECT TO THE DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM, OR THE FG TECHNOLOGY OR LEAD COMPOUNDS, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, ANY WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY, OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, VALIDITY OF FG TECHNOLOGY, PATENTED OR UNPATENTED, AND NONINFRINGEMENT OF THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 'sole obligation' and 'sole remedy' limited to 'replacement' and 'reimbursement' for Lead Compound warranty issues. The changed text broadens the available remedies by including 'any other remedy as chosen by Astellas', thereby directly contradicting the 'sole remedy' clause. Additionally, the disclaimer of warranties in section 15.3 states that, *except as otherwise set forth herein*, FG and Astellas disclaim any other warranties. But the modified version introduces variability by stating 'generally conform' and 'reasonable compliance' in the warranty. This variability impacts the enforcibility of warranty, leading to a direct conflict and contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 Grant to Astellas. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement including Article 12 above, FG hereby grants to Astellas an exclusive license under the FG Technology to: use, package, sell, have sold, import, market and otherwise distribute the Lead Compounds for use solely in the Field in the Astellas Territory
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 Grant to Astellas. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement including Article 12 above, FG hereby grants to Astellas a non-exclusive license under the FG Technology to: use, package, sell, have sold, import, market and otherwise distribute the Lead Compounds for use solely in the Field in the Astellas Territory
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' directly contradicts the original intent. If the license is now non-exclusive, it means that FG can license the same rights to other parties, which undermines Astellas' exclusive position as stated on 8.1, who should have the exclusive right to market, sell and distribute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Astellas. Astellas shall have the exclusive right to market, sell and distribute the Lead Compounds supplied by FG for use in the Astellas Territory within the Field under the license granted in Article 13. Astellas may exercise its rights under this Section 8.1 through one or more Sublicensees; provided, that any such Sublicensee agrees to terms identical in all material respects to those contained in this Agreement, and, provided, further, that any arrangement between Astellas and an Astellas Sublicensee with respect to a Lead Compound shall be subject to the requirements of Section 13.2.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive' in Article 13.1 directly contradicts Article 8.1, which explicitly states that Astellas 'shall have the exclusive right to market, sell and distribute' the Lead Compounds. The non-exclusive license granted in the modified text allows FG to license the same rights to other parties, thus nullifying Astellas' exclusive right as stated in Article 8.1.
